negative_cases.why_wrong: Macro Ifdef Gain Select: keeps the file compilable but forces the observable output path low, so it can pass include/instance smoke checks while failing the required behavior to On each rising clock edge, sample vin unless reset is asserted.

Macro Ifdef Gain Select: keeps the main operator syntax but collapses the computed value through the wrong condition, so coarse smoke cases may run while boundary and waveform checks for Use `ifdef selection to alter a behavioral gain constant fail.
Macro Ifdef Gain Select: introduces incorrect initialization or state bias, so startup and reset-sensitive checks fail while the model still compiles and produces activity.

Macro Ifdef Gain Select: offsets the secondary metric or observable path, so the primary waveform may look plausible while calibrated checker evidence for On each rising clock edge, sample vin unless reset is asserted fails.

Macro Ifdef Gain Select: scales the final observable response, so compile and rough activity checks can pass while calibrated output checks for Use `ifdef selection to alter a behavioral gain constant fail.
